title: AI boom drives an 80% surge in foreign investment bids for Thailand
excerpt: Thailand does not usually feature in the story of the AI boom. This year it does. Foreign firms applied to invest 1.37tn baht, about $40.6bn, in the first six months of 2026, the country’s Board of Investment said on Thursday. That is up 80% on a year earlier. One sector explains most of it. Digital […]
content_html:
Thailand does not usually feature in the story of the AI boom. This year it does. Foreign firms applied to invest 1.37tn baht, about $40.6bn, in the first six months of 2026, the country’s Board of Investment said on Thursday. That is up 80% on a year earlier.

One sector explains most of it. Digital projects, chiefly AI and data centres, drew 1.12tn baht, more than three-quarters of all applications, Bloomberg reported. Across all industries the board logged 1,299 bids, with foreign money targeting 877 projects.

The numbers describe a shift bigger than one country’s ledger. The world’s hyperscalers are hunting for land, power, and permits, and Southeast Asia keeps saying yes. Google, Amazon Web Services, and Microsoft have all announced cloud build-outs in the country. In May the board waved through TikTok’s 842bn-baht data-infrastructure expansion.

What the money is really buying

For Bangkok, the appeal is jobs and status. The approved projects should create more than 82,000 jobs, generate annual exports above 1.24tn baht, and consume 386bn baht of local materials a year, board secretary-general Narit Therdsteerasukdi said.

“Investment value is not the only goal,” he added. “Real success means quality jobs, higher skills and better income for Thai workers.”

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

It also lands at a useful moment. Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has promised to lift an economy trailing neighbours like Indonesia, Malaysia, and Vietnam. War in the Middle East has pushed up energy costs, yet the money kept coming.

“Thailand’s investment growth held steady even as the world economy faced real turbulence,” Narit said, in figures also reported by TNGlobal.

The catch in the pipeline

Two cautions sit under the headline. Applications are not spending, and the board notes not all will come to fruition. Data centres are also hungry for power, which Thailand, like everywhere hosting the regional build-out, will have to find.

Set against the giant projects planned elsewhere and the hundreds of billions China is spending at home, Thailand’s haul is modest. But the direction is unmistakable. The map of where AI physically lives is being redrawn, and Bangkok wants a corner of it.

content_html_zh:
泰国通常不会出现在人工智能繁荣的故事中。 今年确实如此。 该国投资委员会周四表示，2026 年前六个月外国公司申请投资 1.37 兆泰铢，约合 406 亿美元。 比一年前增长了 80%。

一个部门可以解释大部分问题。 据彭博社报道，数字项目，主要是人工智能和数据中心，吸引了 1.12 泰铢，占所有申请的四分之三以上。 该委员会在所有行业共收到了 1,299 个投标，其中外资瞄准了 877 个项目。

这些数字描述的变化比一个国家的账本还要大。 世界各地的超大规模企业都在寻求土地、电力和许可证，而东南亚一直表示愿意。 谷歌、亚马逊网络服务和微软都宣布在该国进行云建设。 5 月，董事会批准了 TikTok 耗资 8420 亿泰铢的数据基础设施扩张计划。

钱真正买的是什么

对于曼谷来说，吸引力在于工作和地位。 委员会秘书长Narit Therdsteerasukdi表示，批准的项目将创造超过82,000个就业机会，年出口额超过1.24兆泰铢，并每年消耗3860亿泰铢的当地材料。

“投资价值并不是唯一的目标，”他补充道。 “真正的成功意味着泰国工人有高质量的工作、更高的技能和更好的收入。”

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

它也落在了一个有用的时刻。 总理阿努丁·查维拉库 (Anutin Charnvirakul) 承诺提振落后于印度尼西亚、马来西亚和越南等邻国的经济。 中东战争推高了能源成本，但资金源源不断。

纳里特在 TNGlobal 也报道的数据中表示：“即使世界经济面临真正的动荡，泰国的投资增长仍保持稳定。”

管道中的陷阱

标题下有两个警告。 申请并不属于支出，董事会指出并非所有申请都会实现。 数据中心也需要电力，而泰国和其他地区扩建项目一样，也必须找到电力。

与其他地方计划的大型项目以及中国在国内花费的数千亿美元相比，泰国的收入微乎其微。 但方向是明确的。 人工智能实际生活的地图正在被重新绘制，曼谷想要占据其中的一角。
